title: Spells UX for Damus iOS: kind:777 as user-friendly custom feeds
description:
## Context

Grimoire "spells" (kind:777 events) encode Nostr REQ filters as portable, shareable events — custom feeds that follow users across clients. Currently only supported by Grimoire (full), Jumble Imwald (cast only), and nak CLI (v0.19.1+, fiatjaf). Formal NIP draft: PR #2244 (NIP-A7). Nostrability tracker: nostrability/nostrability#289.

The challenge: make spells accessible to non-technical iOS users without exposing REQ filters, JSON, or tag encoding. The concept is simple ("saved feed that works in any app") but the current Grimoire UX is a power-user tool.

## Creator feedback (purrgrammer)

> "multi content-type clients that treat every event kind as a first class citizen like Ditto and @Nostria are good candidates. the use case is user-created custom feeds or counts, filterable by you and your contacts' pubkeys (you can do not so common stuff like querying what your contacts are zapping, reacting to, etc.). spells solve the problem of feeds being hardcoded in clients and a closed system. by making REQ queries reusable we make clients open and expose ways to see the network that were not possible before unless client devs hard coded them."

### What this means for Damus

- **The real value prop isn't "custom feeds"** — it's breaking open the closed feed system. Spells let users see the network in ways previously impossible unless a client dev hardcoded that view.
- **"What are my contacts zapping?" "What are they reacting to?"** — these queries exist on the relay wire but no client exposes them because nobody hardcoded that view. Spells make them accessible.
- **Damus is a kind:1-centric client today**, not a multi-content-type client like Ditto or Nostria. The shift isn't just adding a tab bar — it's moving from "Damus shows notes" toward "Damus shows whatever the spell asks for."
- **Rendering arbitrary event kinds** (zaps, reactions, reposts, long-form, etc.) is a harder prerequisite than initially scoped. The visual filter builder needs to expose the interesting stuff (contacts' zaps, reactions), not just the obvious stuff (hashtag + time range).
- **Competitive framing:** clients that don't support spells remain closed systems with hardcoded feeds. Clients that do become open platforms.

## Spec status: kind:777 vs kind:31890

### kind:777 (Spells) — our path

- **Formal NIP draft:** PR #2244 (NIP-A7) by purrgrammer
- **3 independent implementations:** Grimoire (full), nak CLI (fiatjaf), Jumble Imwald (cast)
- **Concept:** portable REQ filter — a single saved query as a Nostr event
- **Tag encoding resolved:** `["tag", "p", pubkey]` is deliberate to avoid relay misindexing (a `["p", pubkey]` top-level tag would cause relays to index the spell as referencing that pubkey). Exception: `["k", "1"]` uses the letter directly to enable relay-side `#k` discovery of spells by kind.
- **Variables:** explicit `$me`, `$contacts` resolved at query time
- **Relative timestamps:** `7d`, `24h`, `1mo` → absolute Unix timestamps at query time
- **Implementation cost:** trivial — parse tags, build one REQ

### kind:31890 (NUD Custom Feeds) — complementary, not competing

- **NIP-FE PR #1554** by staab (Coracle). Original PR #1181 closed without merge.
- **1 implementation:** Coracle (~4 weeks to build the feed compiler)
- **Concept:** composable feed DSL with set operations (union/intersection/difference), WoT scoring, DVM integration, list dereferencing
- **vitorpamplona (Amethyst):** "The only way to ship custom feeds [with kind:31890] is if you implement WOT + DVM + Labels + Replaceables + Lists + Scores + Search at the same time. The kind was made by a super app and only super apps can use it."
- **staab on kind:777:** "This is very nearly the same as [my custom feeds], but without the ability to compose/or/and, or support DVMs."
- **purrgrammer on kind:31890:** "It's intentionally way simpler so it can be implemented in a single REQ with no post-processing. Having to implement DVM just for reusable custom feeds is asking too much."

**Verdict:** kind:777 is the "90% solution" any client can implement with minimal code. kind:31890 is the "full solution" for super apps. They coexist. Implement kind:777 first, add kind:31890 later if demand exists.

## Competitive analysis: Ditto and Nostria

Neither implements kind:777 today. The creator's quote was about them being good candidates, not current support.

### Ditto (server-side Nostr / Mastodon API translation)
- "Every event kind as first class" = server stores all events generically, frontend (Soapbox React) has ~15 purpose-built components. Not a generic system — a large collection of hardcoded renderers.
- No custom feeds. Basic timelines only: home, public, hashtag, suggested.
- Server-side Deno/TypeScript translating Nostr → Mastodon API. Fundamentally different architecture from native iOS. Nothing to borrow.
- Takeaway: "store everything, render what you understand" is what nostrdb already does.

### Nostria (Angular web app + Tauri desktop)
- Has a real custom feed system — closest to what we're building, but proprietary (FeedConfig stored locally + synced via kind:30078 app settings, not shareable between users).
- Kind rendering: hardcoded if-else with 14 specialized components + generic text fallback for unknown kinds. Dispatch duplicated 3x in template.
- **Single active feed model.** Only one feed subscription at a time. Switching feeds tears down previous subscription. Previously had multi-column — removed it.
- No variable resolution ($me/$contacts). Users manually pick people through UI.
- Feed sync: kind:30078 settings events across own devices. Not shareable between users.

### What spells (kind:777) solve that neither has

| Capability | Ditto | Nostria | kind:777 spells |
|-----------|-------|---------|-----------------|
| Custom feeds | None | Proprietary FeedConfig (local only) | Standardized Nostr events |
| Variable resolution | None | None | `$contacts` resolves to each user's follow list at query time. Same spell, different results per user. |
| Feed sharing | Not possible | Not possible | A spell IS a Nostr event — post it, anyone can save it and it works with their contacts automatically. |
| Cross-client portability | N/A | Locked to Nostria | Any client supporting kind:777 can run the same spell |
| Feed sync across devices | N/A | kind:30078 (app settings) | Self-syncing as published Nostr events |

## Approach: native spell execution, gradual kind expansion

Damus will implement spells as native custom feeds — parse kind:777, resolve variables, execute queries, render results. No interim NIP-89 redirect-to-Grimoire step.

### Single active feed model (learned from Nostria)
Only one spell feed subscription active at a time. Selecting a tab subscribes to that feed; previously selected feed unsubscribes and shows cached results. Sidesteps the 14-subscription ceiling in RelayPool. Nostria had multi-column, removed it. Deck UX isn't viable on mobile (validated by TweetDeck, Threads, Nostria).

Scrollable horizontal tabs (Bluesky model) still work — they're a navigation UI, not simultaneous subscriptions.

### Kind support progression
Start with kinds Damus already renders, expand as spell results demand it:

1. **Already supported:** kind:1 (notes), kind:6 (reposts), kind:9735 (zaps), kind:30023 (long-form), kind:9802 (highlights). Note: kind:7 (reactions) are action bar items only — no timeline rendering exists.
2. **Add as needed:** driven by what shows up in popular spells. No upfront commitment to render everything.
3. **Unknown kinds in results:** developer-only feature (behind developer mode toggle). Show generic card with `alt` tag (NIP-31) text and event metadata. Not shown to non-power users.

## Gap analysis — CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / NICE-TO-HAVE

### CRITICAL — feature feels broken without these

#### C1. Empty states (all four scenarios)

NN Group: "Intentionally designed empty states increase user confidence, improve learnability, and facilitate task completion — avoid treating them as afterthoughts."

| Scenario | Design |
|----------|--------|
| Zero saved feeds (new user) | Pre-populate 2-3 starter spell feeds (Bluesky seeds "Following" + "Discover", Spotify auto-generates Discover Weekly). Discovery prompt inline: "Explore custom feeds" |
| Spell returns zero results | "No results found" + plain-language explanation of what was queried + "Try adjusting this feed" action. NN Group: include contextual learning cues |
| $contacts empty (new user) | "This feed shows results from people you follow. Follow some people to get started." + link to suggestions |
| Network down / relay timeout | Show cached results if available: "Offline — showing saved results." No cache: "Can't reach relays. Pull to retry." |

#### C2. Loading and tab switching feel

This IS the core interaction. If switching tabs is slow, nobody uses more than one feed.

- **Tab switch → show cached content instantly** (Bluesky retains last-loaded content in memory per tab)
- **Background refresh starts immediately** on active tab
- **"Load new posts" pill** at top when fresh content arrives — not auto-injected (Bluesky Issue #994: persistent banners were anxiety-inducing, switched to subtle indicator)
- **Pull-to-refresh** as manual override
- **No skeleton screens on return to previously-loaded tab** — cached content is sufficient

#### C3. Feed discovery and bootstrap

Without this, the feature is invisible. Bluesky Issue #873: feed discovery was 4 clicks deep, fixed by surfacing inline + integrating into onboarding (PR #1284).

- **2-3 starter spell feeds pre-pinned** for all users. Same model as Bluesky (seeds defaults) and Spotify (auto-generates from day one). Users never see a bare single-tab experience.
- **[+] button → bottom sheet** with "Discover Feeds" section showing curated/popular spells. Not just "create new" — also "browse existing."
- **In-feed spell cards** are the organic discovery path — someone shares a spell, followers see the card, save it.

#### C4. Spell card in timeline

First interaction most users have with spells. Determines whether the viral loop works.

- Card embedded in poster's note with their commentary
- **Visual hierarchy:** Feed name (bold) → plain-language description → creator attribution → save count (social proof) → "Open" + "Save" actions
- **Anti-ad:** user attribution (real person shared it), organic save count, same visual language as other embeds (link previews, quotes)
- **Save interaction:** lightweight icon toggle (Bluesky +, Spotify heart). Optimistic UI — button changes state immediately. No heavy toast or modal. (NN Group: primary action must be immediately visible)

#### C5. Referenced event resolution for "interesting" queries

The killer examples — "what are my contacts zapping/reacting to" — return referencing events (kind:9735, kind:7). Raw zap receipts are meaningless without the zapped content.

- Spell results containing referencing events (zaps, reactions, reposts) → resolve the referenced event, display it as primary content with the referencing action as context
- "What contacts are zapping" → show the zapped note with "zapped by [contact] — 1000 sats" context
- "What contacts are reacting to" → show the reacted-to note with "liked by [contact]" context
- Requires secondary relay query per unique referenced event (batch and deduplicate)

Without this, the demo of the most compelling spells shows a wall of meaningless receipts.

#### C6. Error states

| Error | Design |
|-------|--------|
| Relay timeout | Timeout after N seconds → "Taking longer than expected. Pull to retry." Show cached if available. |
| Malformed spell | "This feed couldn't be loaded. It may use features not yet supported." |
| Muted content in results | Pass spell results through existing mute/filter pipeline |
| Rate limiting | Backoff + retry. If persistent: "Some relays are limiting requests. Results may be incomplete." |

#### C7. Accessibility

Per AGENTS.md: "44pt touch targets, Dynamic Type, VoiceOver." Per Apple HIG: minimum 44x44pt interactive elements. Per NN Group: tabs need at least two selection indicators.

- **Tab bar:** 44pt minimum height per tab. VoiceOver announces "Feed name, selected, tab, 1 of N." Three-finger swipe to scroll. `accessibilityScrollStatus` for position. Dynamic Type: labels accommodate largest accessibility sizes via horizontal scroll expansion.
- **Selected tab:** underline + bold weight (two indicators per NN Group). Unselected tabs remain visible with good contrast — "overly faded tabs hide content discovery."
- **Spell cards:** "Open Feed" and "Save" buttons need descriptive accessible labels.
- **NN Group on tabs:** single row only (stacked rows destroy spatial memory), labels 1-2 words max, place high-use content first, avoid ALL CAPS.

### IMPORTANT — rough without these, but functional

#### I1. Tab overflow (7+ feeds)

Bluesky Issue #817 → PR #7398: no visual indication tab bar scrolls. Fix: scroll indicator. Material Design: partial item peeking at edges signals scrollability.

Ship with soft limit of ~7 pinned feeds. Add overflow UX (partial peeking, "Manage Feeds" overflow) in Phase 4. Becomes critical as adoption grows.

#### I2. Feed management

- **Remove:** critical day-one. Long-press tab → "Remove Feed" or swipe in management sheet.
- **Reorder:** important for power users. Drag handle in edit mode (Bluesky pattern).
- **Rename:** edge case. Spells have names from their creator.

#### I3. Onboarding / first-encounter education

Twitter Circle pattern: bottom sheet on first encounter. "Maybe Later" skip (softer than "Dismiss"). NN Group: progressive disclosure max 2 levels, show primary features first.

Minimum: bottom sheet on first save or first [+] tap: "Custom Feeds let you see the Nostr network in new ways. Save feeds shared by others, or create your own." One-time, dismissible.

#### I4. Privacy / metadata leak

$contacts sends follow list chunks (500 pubkeys per REQ filter) to relays. Relays learn your social graph. Document in developer settings or "How Custom Feeds Work" info sheet. Power users will notice.

#### I5. Subscribe/unsubscribe churn on iOS

Single-active model = subscribe/unsubscribe on every tab switch. Test: does iOS background suspension tear down WebSocket connections? Reconnection latency could compound. May need connection keepalive or pre-established relay pool.

### NICE-TO-HAVE — polish and future work

#### N1. Animation and transitions
- Tab switch: horizontal swipe paging (Bluesky's `react-native-pager-view` model)
- Save spell → tab appears with subtle slide-in
- Loading → results: fade transition

#### N2. Stale cache indicators
- Subtle "Updated 5m ago" timestamp on cached tabs
- Not critical if background refresh is fast

#### N3. Duplicate/conflict handling
- "You already have this feed" on re-save

#### N4. Spell versioning
- Ship with frozen copies (save = snapshot)
- Add "update available" indicator later

#### N5. Developer-only unknown kind rendering
- Behind developer mode toggle
- `UnknownKindEventView`: alt text, kind number, author, timestamp

#### N6. NIP-89 handler publishing
- Publish kind:31990 for Damus as handler for kind:777 + supported kinds

#### N7. kind:31890 support
- Composable feed DSL for power users. Add if demand exists.

#### N8. kind:778 Lenses
- purrgrammer's proposed extension for parameterized queries. Explicitly future work in spec.

## Phased implementation plan (per AGENTS.md)

Structured as topic branches per [alltheseas AGENTS.md](https://github.com/alltheseas/damus/blob/d5dd5080ff03560af843f51ae7f3a0172e60e52c/AGENTS.md): infrastructure first, features second, polish third. Each commit standalone, builds, passes tests, independently bisectable.

### Phase 0: Spec validation (before code)

- Validate kind:777 against NIP-A7 PR #2244 and Grimoire NIP-xx.md
- Parse real Grimoire kind:777 events from relays to confirm tag format
- Define 2-3 starter spell feeds (curate ourselves or pull popular from relays)
- Confirm referenced event resolution approach for zap/reaction spells

### Phase 1: Infrastructure + critical UX

Core parsing, resolution, subscription management, and the UX foundations that prevent the feature from feeling broken.

**Topic 1: kind:777 event model**
- Add `NostrKind.spell = 777`
- Parse spell tags into `SpellFilter` model: kinds, authors, hashtags, since/until, relay hints
- Variable representation: `$me`, `$contacts` as enum cases
- Relative timestamp representation: `7d`, `24h` as enum
- Unit tests: round-trip parsing of real Grimoire events, malformed/missing tags

**Topic 2: variable + timestamp resolution**
- `$me` → current user pubkey
- `$contacts` → Set<Pubkey> from Contacts (chunked at 500)
- Relative timestamps → absolute Unix timestamps at query time
- Produce `[NostrFilter]` array from `SpellFilter` + resolution context
- Unit tests: various contact list sizes, timestamp edge cases, empty contacts

**Topic 3: spell subscription management (single active)**
- Single active subscription: selecting a spell subscribes, deselecting unsubscribes
- Cached results in per-spell `EventHolder` for instant tab switching
- `SubscriptionManager.advancedStream()` for NDB-first + network parallel
- EOSE isolation: spell feed EOSE independent from home feed
- Unit tests: lifecycle, EOSE sequencing, cache behavior

**Topic 4: referenced event resolution**
- For spell results containing referencing events (kind:9735 zaps, kind:7 reactions, kind:6 reposts): resolve the referenced event via relay query
- Batch and deduplicate referenced event lookups
- Display referenced event as primary content, referencing action as context
- Unit tests: resolution with various reference types, missing references

**Topic 5: empty states, error states, loading states**
- All four empty state scenarios (zero feeds, zero results, empty contacts, network down)
- Error states (timeout, malformed spell, rate limiting)
- Loading states (cached → live transition)
- "Load new posts" pill for incoming content (non-intrusive)
- Unit tests: each state triggers correctly

### Phase 2: UI + first impression

The feature becomes visible and usable. Multi-feed tab bar, spell cards, discovery, accessibility.

**Topic 6: multi-feed tab bar**
- Scrollable `ScrollView(.horizontal)` tab strip above home timeline
- Default tabs: "Following" (existing home feed) + 2-3 starter spell feeds
- [+] button at end → feed management/discovery bottom sheet
- Tab selection switches active subscription (unsubscribes previous, subscribes new)
- Previously selected tabs show cached results instantly
- Auto-pin on save
- Persist tab order and selection locally
- **Accessibility:** 44pt targets, VoiceOver ("Feed name, selected, tab, 1 of N"), `accessibilityScrollStatus`, Dynamic Type accommodation, two selection indicators (underline + bold), good contrast on unselected tabs
- **NN Group compliance:** single row, 1-2 word labels, high-use first, no ALL CAPS

**Topic 7: spell card rendering in timeline**
- `SpellCardView`: feed name, plain-language filter description, creator attribution, save count, "Open" + "Save"
- Plain-language generator: `SpellFilter` → human-readable string
- Anti-ad design: user attribution, organic social proof, consistent embed visual language
- Save = optimistic icon toggle (immediate state change, no toast)
- "Open" → resolve variables, execute, show results in temporary feed view
- "Save" → persist spell + auto-pin to tab bar. Fork: $contacts resolves to YOUR follows.

**Topic 8: feed discovery**
- 2-3 starter feeds pre-pinned (users never see bare single-tab)
- [+] → bottom sheet with "Discover Feeds" section (curated/popular spells) + "Create New"
- Basic feed management: remove (long-press or swipe in management sheet)

### Phase 3: Creation + viral loop

The feature grows. Users create and share spells.

**Topic 9: visual filter builder**
- "What kind of posts?" → kind selector
- "From who?" → People I follow / Everyone / Specific people
- "About?" → hashtag/keyword input
- "How far back?" → time range
- Interesting query templates: "What are my contacts zapping?", "What are they reacting to?"
- Preview results before saving
- Optional "Share" → publish as kind:777 event (the viral loop completes)

**Topic 10: feed management + onboarding**
- Reorder (drag handles in edit mode)
- Rename (edge case)
- First-encounter bottom sheet ("Custom Feeds let you see the Nostr network in new ways...")
- Onboarding flow integration for new users

### Phase 4: Polish + power features

**Topic 11: tab overflow + animation**
- Partial peeking at tab strip edges for scroll affordance
- Horizontal swipe paging between feeds
- Save → tab slide-in animation
- Loading → results fade transition
- Manage Feeds overflow UI for 7+ feeds

**Topic 12: developer + power user features**
- Developer-only unknown kind rendering (alt tag fallback, behind toggle)
- Stale cache indicators
- Privacy documentation
- Spell versioning (frozen copies → "update available")
- Subscribe/unsubscribe churn optimization

**Topic 13: ecosystem integration**
- NIP-89 handler publishing (kind:31990 for Damus)
- kind:31890 support (composable feeds for power users, if demand)
- kind:778 Lenses (if spec matures)

## Risks and architectural constraints

### Hard constraints (from codebase analysis)

1. **~~14-subscription ceiling.~~** **Mitigated by single active feed model.** Only one spell feed subscription at a time. Home feed subscriptions unaffected. $contacts chunking (500 authors per filter) still applies to the active spell but doesn't compound across feeds.

2. **Unknown kinds silently dropped.** `process_event()` switches on `known_kind`. No NIP-31 `alt` tag support exists. Generic fallback is developer-only feature (Phase 4).

3. **kind:7 reactions have no timeline rendering.** Action bar counts only. Spells querying reactions need referenced event resolution (Phase 1, Topic 4) to be useful.

4. **Event rendering is hardcoded if-else.** Consistent with Ditto (15 components) and Nostria (14 components). Hardcoded dispatch is the norm. Each new kind = code PR.

5. **No multi-feed tab bar.** Needs building from scratch (Phase 2, Topic 6).

### Plan-level risks

6. **Stale PR #3392.** Cannot build on it. Cherry-pick selectively or rewrite.

7. **~~Spec maturity.~~** **Largely resolved.** kind:777 has NIP PR #2244, 3 implementations, fiatjaf endorsement via nak. Tag encoding is deliberate, not a disagreement. kind:31890 is complementary.

8. **$contacts at scale.** 1000+ follows → 2-3 subscription chunks for active spell. Single active model prevents compounding. Individual large queries still expensive.

9. **EOSE isolation.** Spell EOSE must be independent from home feed. If spell targets slow relays, must not affect home timeline loading.

10. **Referenced event resolution latency.** "Interesting" spells (zaps, reactions) require secondary queries to fetch the referenced content. Could add significant latency to spell results. Batch and cache aggressively.

11. **iOS background suspension.** Subscribe/unsubscribe churn on tab switch. WebSocket connections may tear down during suspension. Test early (Phase 1).

## Key UX Decisions

### Naming
- Don't call them "spells" in consumer UI — use "Custom Feeds" or "Saved Feeds"
- Interop happens at protocol layer (kind:777), not naming
- Plain-language description: "Notes from people I follow about #bitcoin, last 7 days"

### Receiving a spell (most common first interaction)
- Render kind:777 in feed as a card (Topic 7)
- "Open Feed" resolves $contacts to YOUR contacts, runs query, shows results
- "Save" adds to tab bar (auto-pin, optimistic UI)

### Creating a feed (Topic 9)
- Visual filter builder exposing interesting queries, not just obvious ones
- Preview results before saving
- "Share" publishes as kind:777 (viral loop)

### Forking (viral loop)
- "Save" on someone else's spell = your own copy
- $contacts resolves to YOUR follows, not theirs
- Same recipe, different ingredients

### What to hide from users
- REQ, filter, query, spell, kind terminology
- JSON, tag encoding, relay selection
- Variable resolution mechanics
- Relay negotiation (automatic via outbox)

## UX research sources

### Patterns applied
- **Bluesky:** horizontal tab strip, auto-pin on save, "Discover Feeds" page, starter feeds, cached tabs + background refresh, "Load new posts" pill (Issue #994), scroll affordance (Issue #817 → PR #7398), feed discovery in onboarding (PR #1284)
- **Spotify:** playlist mental model (accept/reject/tune), pre-populated defaults (Discover Weekly), lightweight save (heart icon, optimistic UI)
- **Nostria:** single active subscription (removed multi-column), FeedConfig separation of definition from subscription, cache-first rendering
- **Twitter/X:** bottom sheet onboarding (Circle pattern), "Maybe Later" soft skip
- **NN Group:** empty states (contextual learning cues + action pathways), tabs (single row, 1-2 word labels, two selection indicators, high-use first), progressive disclosure (max 2 levels)
- **Apple HIG:** 44pt touch targets, Dynamic Type, VoiceOver
- **Material Design:** scrollable tabs with partial peeking

### Consumer analogies
- Spotify playlists (shareable, forkable, cross-device)
- Apple Music smart playlists (rule-based, auto-updating)
- Twitter/X Lists (curated people feeds)
- TikTok sounds (tap → see all content using it)

## Related
- kind:777 NIP draft: github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/pull/2244 (NIP-A7)
- kind:31890 NIP draft: github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/pull/1554 (NIP-FE)
- Grimoire source + spec: github.com/purrgrammer/grimoire, NIP-xx.md
- nak spell implementation: github.com/fiatjaf/nak/blob/master/spell.go
- Nostrability tracker: nostrability/nostrability#289
- Outbox tracker: nostrability/nostrability#69
- NIP-89: Recommended Application Handlers
- NIP-31: alt tags
- AGENTS.md: github.com/alltheseas/damus/blob/d5dd5080/AGENTS.md
- Damus PR #3392: grouped view mode (stale, cherry-pick candidates)
- Damus PR #3602: outbox implementation plan
- Damus issue #3624: @alltheseas PR tracker

title: Phase 0: Spec validation for kind:777
description:
Validate kind:777 spec before writing any code.

## Tasks
- [ ] Read NIP-A7 PR #2244 (github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/pull/2244) end-to-end
- [ ] Read Grimoire NIP-xx.md (github.com/purrgrammer/grimoire/blob/main/NIP-xx.md)
- [ ] Fetch real kind:777 events from relays and parse them manually to confirm tag format
- [ ] Confirm tag encoding: ['tag', 'p', pubkey] for filter tags, ['k', '1'] direct for kind discovery
- [ ] Confirm variable format: $me, $contacts in author/tag values
- [ ] Confirm relative timestamp format: 7d, 24h, 1mo
- [ ] Define 2-3 starter spell feeds to ship as defaults (curate or pull popular from relays)
- [ ] Confirm referenced event resolution approach for zap/reaction spell results
- [ ] Document any spec gaps or ambiguities found

## Acceptance
All tag formats validated against real events. Starter feeds defined. No ambiguities remaining.

title: Phase 3, Topic 9: Visual filter builder
description:
User-facing feed creation. The hard UX problem. The viral loop completes here — users create spells and share them.

## Commits (planned)
1. FilterBuilderView: SwiftUI view for creating a spell from UI controls
2. 'What kind of posts?' -> kind selector (Notes, Articles, Pictures, Video) -> ['k', '1']
3. 'From who?' -> People I follow / Everyone / Specific people -> ['authors', '$contacts'] or pubkey picker
4. 'About?' -> hashtag/keyword input -> ['tag', 't', 'bitcoin']
5. 'How far back?' -> time range selector -> ['since', '7d']
6. Interesting query templates (pre-built starting points):
   - 'What are my contacts zapping?' -> kind:9735 + authors:$contacts
   - 'What are my contacts reacting to?' -> kind:7 + authors:$contacts
   - 'What are people I follow bookmarking?' -> kind:30001 + authors:$contacts
7. Preview results: resolve + execute spell, show results before saving
8. Save action: persist locally + auto-pin to tab bar
9. Share action: publish as kind:777 event to relays (the viral loop)
10. SpellFilter -> kind:777 event builder (reverse of parser from Topic 1)

## UX constraints (from research)
- No code, no regex (SkyFeed vs Feed Creator lesson)
- Playlist mental model: 'accept/reject/tune' not 'build from scratch'
- Preview before commit (Bluesky Feed Creator)
- Progressive disclosure: start with templates, expose custom options for power users (NN Group: max 2 levels)

## Acceptance
- Users can create feeds from templates with zero protocol knowledge
- Preview shows actual results before saving
- Share publishes a valid kind:777 event that Grimoire/nak can execute
- Round-trip: create in Damus -> share -> save in Grimoire (and vice versa)

title: Phase 3, Topic 10: Feed management + onboarding
description:
Feed management polish and first-encounter onboarding.

## Feed management
1. Reorder feeds: drag handles in edit mode on feed management sheet (Bluesky pattern)
2. Rename feeds: optional, edge case (spells have names from creator). Tap label to edit.
3. Persist order changes to local storage

## Onboarding
4. First-encounter bottom sheet: triggered on first [+] tap or first spell card encounter
   - 'Custom Feeds let you see the Nostr network in new ways.'
   - 'Save feeds shared by others, or create your own.'
   - 'Maybe Later' soft skip (Twitter Circle pattern, softer than 'Dismiss')
   - One-time, dismissible, stored in UserDefaults
5. Onboarding flow integration: for new users going through initial setup, surface feed selection step (Bluesky PR #1284 model)
6. Progressive disclosure: show primary features first, max 2 levels (NN Group)

## Acceptance
- Feeds can be reordered via drag handles
- First-encounter education shows once and is dismissible
- New users encounter feeds during onboarding, not as a hidden feature

title: Phase 1, Topic 1: kind:777 event model
description:
Parse kind:777 events into a Swift model. No UI. Leans on nostrdb.

## Commits (planned)
1. Add NostrKind.spell = 777 to NostrKind enum
2. SpellFilter model: kinds (k), authors, hashtags (tag), since/until, relay hints
3. Variable representation: $me, $contacts as enum cases (SpellVariable), unresolved
4. Relative timestamp representation: 7d, 24h, 1mo as enum (RelativeTimestamp), unresolved
5. SpellParser: NdbNote -> SpellFilter (read tags, build model)
6. Unit tests: round-trip parsing of real Grimoire kind:777 events
7. Unit tests: malformed/missing tags, edge cases, empty spells

## Acceptance
- SpellFilter model fully represents any valid kind:777 event
- Parser handles all tag types from NIP-A7 spec
- Variables and relative timestamps stored as symbolic values (not resolved yet)
- Tests pass with real Grimoire event fixtures
notes: SpellEvent.swift parser complete with 25 passing tests. NostrKind.spell added. All exhaustive switches updated.

title: Phase 1, Topic 2: Variable + timestamp resolution
description:
Resolve spell variables and relative timestamps into concrete NostrFilter values.

## Commits (planned)
1. SpellResolutionContext: holds current user pubkey + contacts Set<Pubkey>
2. $me resolution -> current user pubkey
3. $contacts resolution -> Set<Pubkey> from Contacts, chunked at MAX_CONTACTS_ON_FILTER (500)
4. Relative timestamp resolution: 7d/24h/1mo -> absolute Unix timestamp at query time
5. SpellResolver: SpellFilter + SpellResolutionContext -> [NostrFilter]
6. Unit tests: resolution with 0, 100, 500, 1500 contacts
7. Unit tests: timestamp edge cases, unresolvable variables (empty contacts)

## Acceptance
- Produces valid [NostrFilter] array from any SpellFilter
- Chunking at 500 authors produces correct number of filter chunks
- Empty $contacts produces empty filter array (not a crash)
- All timestamps resolve to correct absolute values
notes: SpellResolver.swift: resolves $me, $contacts, relative timestamps to concrete NostrFilter. 11 passing tests.

title: Phase 1, Topic 3: Spell subscription management
description:
Execute resolved spell filters as relay subscriptions. Single active feed model.

## Commits (planned)
1. SpellFeedManager: owns the active spell subscription lifecycle
2. Single active subscription: activate(spell) subscribes, deactivate() unsubscribes
3. Per-spell EventHolder cache for instant tab switching
4. Wire to SubscriptionManager.advancedStream() for NDB-first + network parallel
5. EOSE isolation: spell feed EOSE independent from home feed EOSE
6. Subscription teardown on deactivate (clean relay unsubscribe)
7. Unit tests: activate/deactivate lifecycle, EOSE sequencing
8. Unit tests: cache persistence across activate/deactivate cycles

## Acceptance
- Only one spell subscription active at a time
- Switching spells cleanly unsubscribes previous, subscribes new
- Cached results survive deactivation and display instantly on reactivation
- Home feed EOSE unaffected by spell subscriptions
- No subscription leaks (verify with RelayPool subscription count)
notes: SpellFeedModel.swift: ObservableObject managing spell subscriptions via SubscriptionManager.advancedStream. Single-active subscription model.

title: Phase 1, Topic 4: Referenced event resolution
description:
Resolve referenced events in spell results so 'interesting' queries are meaningful.

Spell results for zaps (kind:9735), reactions (kind:7), reposts (kind:6) are references to other events. Raw zap receipts are meaningless without the zapped content. This makes the killer demo work.

## Commits (planned)
1. SpellResultEvent wrapper: holds the spell result event + optional resolved referenced event
2. Reference extraction: kind:9735 -> zapped event id, kind:7 -> reacted-to event id, kind:6 -> reposted event id
3. Batch reference resolver: collect unique referenced event ids, query nostrdb first, relay fallback for misses
4. Deduplicate: multiple zaps on same note -> one relay query
5. Display model: referenced event as primary content, spell result event as context overlay ('zapped by X - 1000 sats', 'liked by X')
6. Unit tests: resolution for each reference type, missing references, batch dedup

## Acceptance
- 'What are my contacts zapping' shows the zapped CONTENT with zap context, not raw receipts
- 'What are my contacts reacting to' shows the reacted-to CONTENT with reaction context
- nostrdb cache hit avoids relay queries for already-stored events
- Missing referenced events show graceful fallback (not crash)

title: Phase 1, Topic 5: Empty states, error states, loading states
description:
All critical UX states that prevent the feature from feeling broken.

## Empty states (NN Group: contextual learning cues + action pathways)
- Zero saved feeds: pre-populated starter feeds handle this (Phase 2), but need the empty state view as fallback
- Zero results from spell: 'No results found' + plain-language explanation of query + 'Try adjusting this feed' action
- Empty $contacts: 'This feed shows results from people you follow. Follow some people to get started.' + link to suggestions
- Network down: cached results with 'Offline — showing saved results.' No cache: 'Can't reach relays. Pull to retry.'

## Error states
- Relay timeout: timeout after N seconds -> 'Taking longer than expected. Pull to retry.' Show cached if available.
- Malformed spell: 'This feed couldn't be loaded. It may use features not yet supported.'
- Rate limiting: backoff + retry. If persistent: 'Some relays are limiting requests. Results may be incomplete.'

## Loading states
- Tab switch: cached content instantly, background refresh in parallel
- 'Load new posts' pill when fresh content arrives (non-intrusive, not auto-injected per Bluesky Issue #994)
- Pull-to-refresh as manual override
- Initial load (no cache): centered spinner with timeout fallback

## Acceptance
- Every state has a designed view (no blank screens, no infinite spinners)
- Empty states include contextual help and action pathways
- Error states are graceful and actionable
- Loading transitions feel instant for cached content
notes: SpellFeedView.swift: idle/loading/loaded/error states. SpellFeedLoadingView, SpellFeedEmptyView, SpellFeedErrorView with retry.

title: Phase 2, Topic 6: Multi-feed tab bar
description:
Scrollable horizontal tabs above the home timeline. Single active feed — tab selection switches the active subscription. The biggest UI change.

## Commits (planned)
1. FeedTabBar view: ScrollView(.horizontal) tab strip component
2. Tab model: id, label (1-2 words per NN Group), icon, associated spell (or nil for Following)
3. Default tabs: 'Following' (existing home feed) + starter spell feeds
4. Tab selection state: switches active SpellFeedManager subscription
5. Tab visual design: underline + bold for selected (two indicators per NN Group), good contrast on unselected
6. [+] button at end of tab strip -> placeholder for discovery sheet (Topic 8)
7. Persist tab order and selection in local storage
8. Wire cached content display on inactive tabs (instant switch from Topic 3)
9. Accessibility: 44pt touch targets, VoiceOver ('Feed name, selected, tab, 1 of N'), accessibilityScrollStatus, Dynamic Type accommodation
10. Integration with existing MainTabView / home timeline

## Design constraints (NN Group + Apple HIG)
- Single row only (stacked rows destroy spatial memory)
- Labels: 1-2 words max, sentence/title case, no ALL CAPS
- High-use content (Following) first and selected by default
- Unselected tabs visible with good contrast
- 44pt minimum height per tab
- Dynamic Type: horizontal scroll accommodates text expansion

## Acceptance
- Tab strip renders above home timeline with Following + starter feeds
- Switching tabs instantly shows cached content, starts background subscription
- Only one subscription active at a time
- VoiceOver fully navigable (three-finger swipe, position announcements)
- Dynamic Type at largest size does not break layout

title: Phase 2, Topic 7: Spell card rendering in timeline
description:
Render kind:777 events encountered in timeline as actionable cards. This is the first interaction most users have with spells — the viral loop entry point.

## Commits (planned)
1. SpellCardView: SwiftUI view for kind:777 events in timeline
2. Visual hierarchy: feed name (bold) -> plain-language description -> creator attribution -> save count -> 'Open' + 'Save' actions
3. Plain-language generator: SpellFilter -> human-readable string ('Notes from people I follow about #bitcoin, last 7 days')
4. Save interaction: optimistic icon toggle (immediate state change, no toast). Follows Bluesky +/Spotify heart pattern.
5. 'Open Feed' action: resolve variables, execute spell, show results in temporary feed view
6. 'Save' action: persist spell locally + auto-pin to tab bar (auto-pin per Bluesky lesson)
7. Fork behavior: saving someone else's spell resolves $contacts to YOUR follows
8. Anti-ad design: user attribution, organic save count, same visual language as other embeds
9. Wire into EventView.swift: add kind:777 branch before TextEvent fallback
10. Accessible labels on Open/Save buttons

## Acceptance
- kind:777 events render as cards in timeline, not raw text
- Plain-language description is accurate and readable
- Save is one tap, optimistic, auto-pins to tab bar
- Card doesn't look like an ad (attribution, social proof, consistent embed style)
- Open shows spell results with resolved variables
notes: SpellCardView.swift: EventShell-wrapped card with badges. Added to EventView.swift and all 3 compile targets.

title: Phase 2, Topic 8: Feed discovery and bootstrap
description:
Make the feature visible. Without discovery, spells are invisible to users.

Bluesky Issue #873: feed discovery was 4 clicks deep. Fixed by surfacing inline + integrating into onboarding (PR #1284). We must not repeat this mistake.

## Commits (planned)
1. Starter spell feeds: 2-3 curated spells pre-pinned for all users (defined in Phase 0)
2. First-run seeding: on first launch (or first update with spells), add starter feeds to tab bar
3. Discovery bottom sheet: triggered by [+] button on tab strip
4. 'Discover Feeds' section in sheet: curated/popular spell cards (query relays for kind:777 events with high engagement)
5. 'Create New' entry point in sheet (placeholder for Phase 3 filter builder)
6. Basic feed management in sheet: list saved feeds with remove action (swipe or long-press)

## Discovery sources
- Curated starter set (hardcoded spell event data, updated with app releases)
- Popular spells from relays: query kind:777 events, sort by engagement (zaps, reactions)
- In-feed spell cards (Topic 7) as organic discovery

## Acceptance
- New users see 2-3 feeds beyond 'Following' on first launch
- [+] button opens discovery with browsable spell options
- Users can remove saved feeds
- Feature is discoverable within 1 tap from home screen ([+] button)

title: Rework SpellResolver to use ndb_filter (NdbFilter) as foundation
description:
jb55 suggests using nfilter (nostrdb NdbFilter) as the internal foundation for spell feed resolution, keeping kind:777 spells as the interop layer between Damus and other Nostr apps.

## Current state
SpellResolver produces [NostrFilter] passed to advancedStream. The subscription system converts NostrFilter to NdbFilter under the hood for relay queries. We do not leverage nostrdb local filtering.

## What needs to change
1. SpellResolver should produce NdbFilter directly (or a wrapper with both local and relay query paths)
2. SpellFeedModel should query local nostrdb first using ndb_filter for instant results, then supplement with relay results
3. Hybrid local+relay approach gives instant display of cached events while new ones stream in
4. kind:777 remains the wire/sharing format. NdbFilter is the internal execution format

## Key files
- nostrdb/NdbFilter.swift: Swift wrapper around ndb_filter
- damus/Features/Spells/Models/SpellResolver.swift: currently produces NostrFilter, needs to produce NdbFilter
- damus/Features/Spells/Models/SpellFeedModel.swift: subscription management, needs local query path
- nostrdb/Ndb.swift: local database query interface

## Reference
- PR comment: https://github.com/damus-io/damus/pull/3711#issuecomment-4099820225
- NdbFilter.swift converts NostrFilter to ndb_filter via C interop
- Ndb.swift has query methods that accept ndb_filter
